summary: Tested educator apply deduplication fix, admin panel, and educator funnel page. Primary fix (re-apply updates existing record instead of creating duplicates) is working correctly. 11/13 backend tests pass. 2 minor response-level bugs found in the new application path.

minor.issue: New application (first-time) returns status='' instead of status='new'. Root cause: sanitize_nullable_fields model validator in EducatorApplication sets status='' when input dict (from EducatorApplicationCreate.model_dump()) doesn't include 'status' key, overriding the Pydantic default 'new'. Fix: After 'application = EducatorApplication(**data.model_dump())', add 'if not application.status: application.status = "new"'. OR remove 'status' from str_fields in sanitize_nullable_fields.

New application (first-time) response returns meeting_link='' even though DB stores the correct link. Root cause: code sets meeting_link on 'doc' dict (stored to DB) but returns 'application' Pydantic object whose .meeting_link attribute is never set. Fix: Add 'application.meeting_link = meeting_link' before 'return application'. The re-apply path correctly returns meeting_link because it fetches from DB.
Email notification error: 'GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD is not defined' — non-critical background task failure. Emails won't send but API calls succeed. Pre-existing issue unrelated to this fix.

action_items: Fix new application response: set application.status = 'new' explicitly in create_educator_application when no status is provided (line ~613 in educators.py)
Fix new application response: set application.meeting_link = meeting_link before returning (line ~638 in educators.py)
Optional cleanup: dev test records with phones 9999000001, 9999000002 and TEST_* phones (8888100001-8888900002) can be removed from DB
critical_code_review_comments: In EducatorApplication.sanitize_nullable_fields: 'status' is in str_fields, so when creating a NEW application from EducatorApplicationCreate (which has no 'status' field), values.get('status') returns None and gets set to '' — overriding the model field default 'new'. This makes new applications show up with empty status in API responses. The sanitizer should not override fields that have meaningful defaults.

In create_educator_application: meeting_link is set on 'doc' dict but NOT on 'application' object before return. So API response always has empty meeting_link for new applications even though DB stores it correctly.

success_rate.backend: 85% (11/13 tests pass; 2 minor failures are bugs in code, not test issues)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (educator funnel page loads, admin panel loads with all tabs, applicants list shows no duplicates)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ educator records with phones 8888100001-8888900002 during testing. Multiple re-apply submissions confirmed only 1 record per phone exists.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Primary fix tested: deduplication on re-apply works (re-apply with same phone or email UPDATES existing record, not creates duplicate). Admin panel /admin/educators loads with all tabs (Requirements 2, Applicants 20, Onboarding 15, Active Educators 5, Archived 3). 2 minor response bugs: (1) status='' instead of 'new' for new apps (2) meeting_link='' in response even though stored in DB. Previous NameError for send_educator_application_received_email is now fixed. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter70_educator_apply.py

rca_of_issues.duplicate_registration_fix: VERIFIED FIXED - the re-apply path in /educators/apply now correctly calls update_one instead of returning stale data or creating a new record. Confirmed by: (1) test_reapply_same_phone_no_duplicate_created (3 submissions = 1 DB record), (2) test_reapply_same_email_no_duplicate, (3) visual verification in admin panel showing unique phone appears exactly once.
rca_of_issues.status_empty_bug: Root cause: EducatorApplication.sanitize_nullable_fields model validator includes 'status' in str_fields. When EducatorApplicationCreate (which has no status field) is model_dump()'d, 'status' key is absent. The validator then sets values['status'] = '' because values.get('status') is None. This overrides the Pydantic field default of 'new'. Only affects the API response — the admin panel still shows correct data since EducatorApplicationCreate path doesn't set demo_scheduled either (demo_scheduled IS correctly set explicitly).
rca_of_issues.meeting_link_empty_bug: Root cause: create_educator_application creates 'application' Pydantic object, then creates 'doc' dict from model_dump(), sets doc['meeting_link'] = meeting_link (saves to DB correctly), but returns 'application' object which still has meeting_link=''. The re-apply path correctly returns meeting_link because it reads from DB after update.
